reasoning: NASA has officially delayed the Artemis III mission, which will be the first return of humans to the lunar surface, until September 2026 at the earliest. Given the complexity of the mission and the likelihood of further delays (such as delays in SpaceX's Starship HLS development and spacesuit design), it is highly improbable that a human moon landing will happen by the end of 2025. China's manned moon landing is not planned until 2030. Therefore, the chance of a 2025 moon landing is less than 2%.
